source_note: Numbers sourced from California state rebuilding dashboard, which aggregates official data from each jurisdiction. The City of Los Angeles section reflects Palisades Fire rebuilding within City limits.

commercial.as_of_note: Per Council Member Traci Park, early 2026. Includes library, rec center, and YMCA in active planning. No destroyed commercial buildings have been fully rebuilt yet — Gelson's broke ground first in spring 2026.

businesses.name: CVS Pharmacy
businesses.detail: First major retailer to return. Building was damaged, not destroyed — fully repaired and expanded. Now serves first responders and construction workers as well as residents.

businesses.name: Chipotle
businesses.detail: Among the earliest national chains to return. Building was damaged, repaired.

businesses.name: Anawalt Lumber
businesses.detail: Family-owned hardware store, vital for rebuilding effort. Returned among the first wave of reopenings.

businesses.name: Village General Store
businesses.detail: One of the first businesses to reopen after the fire — by mid-February 2025, only weeks after the blaze.

businesses.name: Pacific West General Store
businesses.detail: Gas station and convenience store on PCH. Connected Taco Bell reopened March 17, 2025.

businesses.name: Moku Sushi
businesses.detail: Local sushi spot reopened April 1, 2025 in the Highlands area.

businesses.name: Spruzzo Palisades
businesses.detail: Italian restaurant in the Highlands. Owner reports sales still below pre-fire levels but business confidence growing.

businesses.name: Gladstones
businesses.detail: Iconic PCH seafood spot reopened deck for limited dining on July 4, 2025. Full interior renovation still underway. Saved from total loss by LAFD Engine 238.

businesses.name: Duke's Malibu
businesses.detail: Reopened after fourteen months of recovery from fire damage and subsequent mudslides.

businesses.name: Gelson's
businesses.detail: Announced March 2026 that they will return to longtime location. Construction barriers now up. No reopening date set — likely 2027+.

businesses.name: Ralphs
businesses.detail: Destroyed in fire. Kroger has stated intent to return but no construction timeline announced.

businesses.name: Palisades Village
businesses.detail: Caruso's open-air retail center. $60M renovation; tenants expected to open August 2026. Largely untouched by fire structurally but closed due to surrounding devastation.

businesses.name: Erewhon
businesses.detail: Returning as anchor grocer, café and tonic bar at Palisades Village. Opens with the center in August 2026.

businesses.name: Spacca Tutto
businesses.detail: New Italian steakhouse from Nancy Silverton and Rick Caruso, anchoring Palisades Village. First-time concept opening with the center.

businesses.name: Business Block (Cosa Nostra & Starbucks)
businesses.detail: Historic Spanish Colonial Revival complex destroyed in fire. Construction barricades up; owner rebuilding. Cosa Nostra Trattoria currently focused on Westlake location.

businesses.name: Palisades Plaza (Chase Bank)
businesses.detail: Chase Bank-anchored office complex destroyed in fire. Rebuild status uncertain.

businesses.name: Reel Inn
businesses.detail: Lease terminated by California state due to licensing issues post-fire. Iconic seafood shack will not return at original location.

businesses.name: Cholada Thai
businesses.detail: Lease terminated alongside Reel Inn. Long-standing PCH Thai institution will not return.

civic_infrastructure.name: Palisades Charter High School
civic_infrastructure.detail: Students returned to campus January 27, 2026 — over 30% of classrooms had been destroyed. Currently operating with repaired and temporary facilities. Permanent rebuild targeted for 2028 as part of $604M LAUSD project.

civic_infrastructure.name: Marquez Charter Elementary School
civic_infrastructure.detail: Original campus burned to the ground. Classes have been held in temporary modular buildings since September 2025. Permanent rebuild expected by 2028.

civic_infrastructure.name: Palisades Charter Elementary School
civic_infrastructure.detail: Damaged in fire. Part of LAUSD's $604M three-school Pacific Palisades rebuild project. Permanent campus expected 2028, funded by Measure US bond.

civic_infrastructure.name: Palisades Recreation Center
civic_infrastructure.detail: Public-private partnership announced April 2025 (LA Strong Sports, Steadfast LA, others) to fully rebuild. Groundbreaking January 2026. New playground opened July 2025 as interim community space.

civic_infrastructure.name: Palisades Library (LAPL)
civic_infrastructure.detail: Temporary library facility planned to open near old rec center in early 2026 — computers, study spaces, Wi-Fi, printing, community rooms. Permanent rebuild on original site in progress.

civic_infrastructure.name: Lowe Family YMCA
civic_infrastructure.detail: Destroyed in fire after 60+ years serving Palisades-Malibu families. $25M capital campaign launched summer 2025; Bank of America gave first major $1M grant January 2026. Two new campuses planned: original Via de la Paz site + nearby Simon Meadow. Groundbreaking spring 2026.

civic_infrastructure.name: Palisades Playground
civic_infrastructure.detail: New playground opened mid-late July 2025 with ribbon-cutting. Serves as interim community gathering space while the rec center is rebuilt.

help_resources.name: City of LA One-Stop Permit Center
help_resources.desc: In-person permit and rebuilding paperwork help. Open Mon–Fri 10am–8pm, Sat–Sun 10am–4pm.

help_resources.name: LA County One-Stop Permit Centers
help_resources.desc: Walk-in support in Altadena and Calabasas. Includes rebuild consultations and SCE appointments.

help_resources.name: LADBS Pre-Approved Standard Plan Pilot Program
help_resources.desc: Library of pre-approved single-family home designs that skip full plan check. Can shave 10%+ off rebuild cost.

help_resources.name: Archistar AI Plan Check Tool
help_resources.desc: Free AI-powered plan review — upload your rebuilding plans for instant feedback before submitting to City/County for final approval.

help_resources.name: EPIC-LA — LA County Online Permitting
help_resources.desc: Apply for rebuilding permits online, request inspections, determine what permits you need for your property.

help_resources.name: LADWP Palisades Inspections & Permitting Support
help_resources.desc: Utility-specific support center for power, water, electrical panel upgrades, and meter spots.

milestones.title: Palisades Fire ignites
milestones.detail: Wind-driven fire begins near Temescal Ridge; rapidly spreads through Pacific Palisades, parts of Malibu, and Topanga.

milestones.title: Mayor Bass signs Executive Order 1
milestones.detail: Suspends permit fees; waives discretionary review for like-for-like rebuilds; sets up streamlined LADBS pathway.

milestones.title: First business reopens
milestones.detail: Village General Store on Sunset Blvd becomes the first Pacific Palisades business to reopen post-fire — just five weeks after the blaze.

milestones.title: First rebuild permit issued
milestones.detail: Day 57 post-fire — roughly twice as fast as the first permits issued after the Camp and Woolsey fires.

milestones.title: One-Stop Rebuilding Center opens
milestones.detail: Permit assistance hub at 1828 W. Sawtelle Blvd. consolidates LADBS, Planning, Bureau of Engineering, and DWP intake.

milestones.title: Debris removal substantially complete
milestones.detail: USACE and contractors finish clearance on nearly all parcels in the City of LA portion of the burn area — nearly a year ahead of original expectations.

milestones.title: AB 130 and SB 131 signed
milestones.detail: State CEQA reform shortens agency review timelines, expands Permit Streamlining Act, adds near-miss review.

milestones.title: New Palisades playground opens
milestones.detail: Interim community space at the Rec Center site — first piece of civic infrastructure to come back online.

milestones.title: CVS first major retailer to reopen
milestones.detail: Pharmacy on Swarthmore Ave is first national chain to fully return. Building was repaired rather than rebuilt.

milestones.title: Standard Plan Pilot Program launches
milestones.detail: Library of pre-approved single-family designs — homeowners can skip full plan check by selecting a stock plan.

milestones.title: First certificate of occupancy issued
milestones.detail: Showcase home built by Thomas James Homes at 915 N Kagawa St becomes the first rebuilt home to be legally cleared for move-in. LADBS crosses 1,000 issued permits the same month.

milestones.title: LA County dashboard upgraded
milestones.detail: Permitting Progress Dashboard relaunched with color-coded views and parcel-level detail across 12,000+ projects.

milestones.title: One-year anniversary · First homeowner CofO
milestones.detail: On the fire's anniversary, Cover delivers the first ground-up rebuild to an actual homeowner — Sue Labella, a 48-year Palisades resident — using all-steel non-combustible panels. Across both fires: 2,600+ residential permits issued.

milestones.title: Bank of America commits $1M to YMCA rebuild
milestones.detail: First major corporate grant to the Lowe Family YMCA's $25M capital campaign. Groundbreaking targeted for spring 2026 across two campuses.

milestones.title: Palisades Charter High School returns to campus
milestones.detail: Students and staff return to repaired and temporary facilities one year after the fire. 30% of classrooms had been destroyed.

milestones.title: City Council waives rebuild fees
milestones.detail: LA City Council unanimously approves full waiver of permit and plan check fees for fire-impacted private property.

milestones.title: Gelson's announces rebuild
milestones.detail: Upscale grocer commits to returning to longtime Sunset Blvd location, becoming the first major destroyed retail building to enter active rebuild.

milestones.title: Property tax payment deadline
milestones.detail: Final day for fire-impacted property owners to make payments under the modified assessment program.

milestones.title: LA County establishes Disaster Recovery Rebuild Authority
milestones.detail: Board of Supervisors votes unanimously to create a new authority to coordinate long-term rebuilding efforts across the Palisades and Eaton fire footprints, housed within the Department of Public Works.

resources.name: LADBS Palisades Rebuild Portal
resources.desc: City of LA Department of Building & Safety — hourly-updated permit dashboard

resources.name: LA County Permitting Progress Dashboard
resources.desc: County tracker for both Palisades and Eaton fire zones — refreshes every 3 hours

resources.name: LA Strong: Return & Rebuild
resources.desc: City of LA recovery hub — debris removal, ROE submissions, final inspections

resources.name: California Rebuilding Dashboard
resources.desc: State-level dashboard covering LA City, LA County, Malibu, and Pasadena

resources.name: Pali Builds
resources.desc: Independent, privately operated rebuild data tracker by local builders

resources.name: Palisadian-Post Rebuild Dashboard
resources.desc: Weekly published dashboard using Pali Builds data, updated April 30, 2026
